title: Code of Conduct
text: reportilo health research software project. expect contributors users keep discussion professional, evidence-based, respectful. Examples behavior helps project: Use welcoming inclusive language. Focus criticism code, data, documentation, methods, people. State uncertainty clearly discussing reporting guidance data quality. Respect different levels experience R, Shiny, React, research reporting guidelines. Accept maintainers’ decisions scope, release readiness, moderation. Examples unacceptable behavior: Harassment, threats, insults, discriminatory language. Sexualized language imagery. Personal attacks, trolling, sustained disruption. Public private disclosure another person’s private information. Knowingly misleading claims guideline coverage, checklist validation, research reporting standards.

title: Code of Conduct
text: code conduct applies project spaces, including GitHub issues, pull requests, discussions, code review, documentation, public private communication project.

title: Code of Conduct
text: Instances unacceptable behavior may reported project maintainer email address listed DESCRIPTION. Reports reviewed promptly confidentially practical. Maintainers may remove comments, close issues, reject contributions, block participants needed protect project community.

title: Security Policy
text: Security fixes handled default branch. reportilo stable release series, current development version supported.

title: Security Policy
text: Please open public issue suspected security vulnerability. Report privately maintainer email listed DESCRIPTION. Include: affected package app surface: R package, Shiny app, browser app, data pipeline, documentation site, GitHub Actions; steps reproduce; expected observed behavior; whether issue can expose local files, execute code, exfiltrate data, corrupt exports, misrepresent checklist data; package version, commit SHA, browser, operating system, R Node version. maintainer acknowledge report practical, assess severity, coordinate fix public disclosure warranted.

title: Security Policy
text: Please take extra care : browser save/load JSON validation; SVG, Graphviz DOT, Word, Excel, CSV, image exports; CSV formula neutralization; Shiny file downloads rendered HTML; downloaded guideline source files data-pipeline caches; GitHub Actions credentials deployment workflows.

title: Security Policy
text: browser dependency advisories, distinguish production dependencies development toolchain advisories. Production dependency vulnerabilities block deployment resolved explicitly assessed.

title: Filling in reporting checklists
text: get_checklist() (alias new_checklist()) returns fillable checklist guideline: one row per item, empty response column. object carries guideline id, title whether checklist hand-verified:
code: chk <- get_checklist("prisma-2020") class(chk) #> [1] "reportilo_checklist" "data.frame" nrow(chk) #> [1] 42 head(chk[, c("item_no", "section", "item_text")]) #>   item_no section      #> 1 1       Title        #> 2 2       Abstract     #> 3 3       Introduction #> 4 4       Introduction #> 5 5       Methods      #> 6 6       Methods      #>   item_text                                                                                                                                                                      #> 1 Identify the report as a systematic review.                                                                                                                                    #> 2 See the PRISMA 2020 for Abstracts checklist.                                                                                                                                   #> 3 Describe the rationale for the review in the context of existing knowledge.                                                                                                    #> 4 Provide an explicit statement of the objective(s) or question(s) the review addresses.                                                                                         #> 5 Specify the inclusion and exclusion criteria for the review and how studies were grouped for the syntheses.                                                                    #> 6 Specify all databases, registers, websites, organizations, reference lists and other sources searched or consulted; specify the date when each was last searched or consulted. attr(chk, "guideline_id") #> [1] "prisma-2020" attr(chk, "verified") #> [1] TRUE

title: Filling in reporting checklists
text: response ordinary character column. Record page number (note) item addressed.
code: chk$response[chk$item_no == "1"] <- "p1" chk$response[chk$item_no == "16a"] <- "p7, Figure 1"  validate_checklist(chk) #> prisma-2020: 2 of 42 items completed.

title: Filling in reporting checklists
text: main families ship hand-verified checklists. Many guidelines auto-extracted source documents; treat starting point check original. Use parse_status see .
code: ps <- parse_status head(ps[ps$verified, c("guideline_id", "n_items", "status")]) #>    guideline_id n_items    status #> 19      consort      37 parsed_ok #> 53  prisma-2020      42 parsed_ok #> 86       strobe      22 parsed_ok  # guidelines flagged for review (lower-confidence auto-extraction) sum(ps$needs_review) #> [1] 33

title: Getting started with reportilo
text: Get fillable checklist. row one item; record address response column. Flow diagrams work way. Start template, set counts, view .
code: chk <- get_checklist("strobe") head(chk) #> <reportilo checklist> strobe (verified) #> The Strengthening the Reporting of Observational Studies in Epidemiology (STROBE) Statement: guidelines for reporting observational studies #>   item_no section            #> 1 1       Title and abstract #> 2 2       Introduction       #> 3 3       Introduction       #> 4 4       Methods            #> 5 5       Methods            #> 6 6       Methods            #>   item_text                                                                                                                                                                                            #> 1 Indicate the study's design with a commonly used term in the title or the abstract; provide in the abstract an informative and balanced summary of what was done and what was found.                 #> 2 Explain the scientific background and rationale for the investigation being reported.                                                                                                                #> 3 State specific objectives, including any prespecified hypotheses.                                                                                                                                    #> 4 Present key elements of study design early in the paper.                                                                                                                                             #> 5 Describe the setting, locations, and relevant dates, including periods of recruitment, exposure, follow-up, and data collection.                                                                     #> 6 Give the eligibility criteria, and the sources and methods of selection of participants; describe methods of follow-up (cohort), matching (case-control/cohort), or case ascertainment and controls. #>   response #> 1 <NA>     #> 2 <NA>     #> 3 <NA>     #> 4 <NA>     #> 5 <NA>     #> 6 <NA>  chk$response[1:3] <- c("p1", "p2", "p2") validate_checklist(chk) #> strobe: 3 of 22 items completed. fc <- new_flowchart("prisma_2020") fc <- set_counts(fc, identified_db = 1200, screened = 980, excluded = 700) fc #> <reportilo flowchart> PRISMA 2020 flow diagram (prisma_2020) #>  field            label                                           #>  identified_db    Records identified from databases and registers #>  duplicates       Duplicate records removed                       #>  auto_removed     Records marked ineligible by automation tools   #>  other_removed    Records removed for other reasons               #>  screened         Records screened                                #>  excluded         Records excluded                                #>  sought           Reports sought for retrieval                    #>  not_retrieved    Reports not retrieved                           #>  assessed         Reports assessed for eligibility                #>  reports_excluded Reports excluded (with reasons)                 #>  studies_included Studies included in review                      #>  reports_included Reports of included studies                     #>  value                              #>  1200                               #>  0                                  #>  0                                  #>  0                                  #>  980                                #>  700                                #>  0                                  #>  0                                  #>  0                                  #>  Reason 1 (n = 0); Reason 2 (n = 0) #>  0                                  #>  0                                  #> Render with render_flowchart() or export with reportilo_export(). render_flowchart(fc)

Coverage partial extracted checklists verified; reportilo_coverage() reports verified versus extracted, category. Data-driven flow diagram templates: PRISMA 2020, CONSORT STARD, plus observational designs (cohort, case-control cross-sectional). title: Check a flow diagram for count inconsistencies — flowchart_consistency
text: Apply template-specific accounting bounds, including flow invariants template explicit removal/exclusion counts: example PRISMA screened exceed identified - duplicates - automation - , CONSORT randomized exceed assessed - excluded. Reason fields ignored.

title: Check a flow diagram for count inconsistencies — flowchart_consistency
Check a flow diagram for count inconsistencies — flowchart_consistency
text: x reportilo_flowchart. complete TRUE, also flag conservation stages count less (records unaccounted ), stages exceeds bound. Use final, fully filled diagram. Default FALSE (bounds ), suits draft.

title: Check a flow diagram for count inconsistencies — flowchart_consistency
text: character vector issue messages (empty counts consistent).

title: Check a flow diagram for count inconsistencies — flowchart_consistency
text: default bounds checked (strict equality), partially filled draft flagged. Set complete = TRUE finished diagram additionally require exact accounting every conservation stage: stage whose removals/exclusions fully specified (inflowing count must equal base minus removals) split-stage (parts must sum whole, e.g. CONSORT randomized = intervention arm + control arm). catches records silently unaccounted otherwise complete diagram.

title: Check a flow diagram for count inconsistencies — flowchart_consistency
code: fc <- set_counts(new_flowchart("prisma_2020"), identified_db = 100, screened = 200) flowchart_consistency(fc) #> [1] "Records screened (200) exceeds Records identified from databases and registers - Duplicate records removed - Records marked ineligible by automation tools - Records removed for other reasons (100)."  # complete mode catches under-accounting that bounds mode allows draft <- set_counts(new_flowchart("prisma_2020"),   identified_db = 200, duplicates = 50, screened = 100) flowchart_consistency(draft) # ok within bounds #> character(0) flowchart_consistency(draft, complete = TRUE) # 50 records unaccounted #> [1] "Records screened (100) is less than Records identified from databases and registers - Duplicate records removed - Records marked ineligible by automation tools - Records removed for other reasons (150); 50 record(s) unaccounted." #> [2] "Reports sought for retrieval (0) is less than Records screened - Records excluded (100); 100 record(s) unaccounted."

what: reportilo: Fill in and Export EQUATOR Reporting Guidelines and Flow Diagrams — reportilo-package
title: reportilo: Fill in and Export EQUATOR Reporting Guidelines and Flow Diagrams — reportilo-package
text: toolkit health research reporting guidelines curated EQUATOR Network. 'reportilo' bundles searchable catalog full EQUATOR guideline library plus machine-readable checklists subset guidelines: small hand-verified core larger best-effort, automatically extracted set whose extraction status confidence recorded (see reportilo_coverage() parse_status dataset). also provides data-driven flow diagram templates (PRISMA 2020, CONSORT, STARD common observational designs) risk--bias plots. Users fill checklist responses, flow diagram counts risk--bias judgments export result Word, Excel image files. bundled 'shiny' application provides point--click front end, data powers companion browser application. Auto-extracted checklists verified original guideline use.

title: reportilo: Fill in and Export EQUATOR Reporting Guidelines and Flow Diagrams — reportilo-package
text: EQUATOR Network (Enhancing QUAlity Transparency health Research) maintains canonical library reporting guidelines health research. reportilo turns library working toolkit: searchable catalog every guideline, fillable checklists subset (small hand-verified core plus best-effort, automatically extracted data), data-driven flow diagram templates, risk--bias plots. Checklist coverage partial reported reportilo_coverage(). package organized around three verbs: Find guideline reportilo_guidelines(), search_guidelines() guideline_info(). Fill checklist get_checklist() / new_checklist(), flow diagram new_flowchart() set_counts(). Export filled object Word, Excel image reportilo_export(). data rendering logic kept separate user interface checklists flow diagrams drive package functions, bundled Shiny application (launch_reportilo()) companion browser application.

title: reportilo: Fill in and Export EQUATOR Reporting Guidelines and Flow Diagrams — reportilo-package
text: Guideline metadata derived EQUATOR Network reporting guideline library. Checklist items extracted guideline source documents; item records provenance parse-confidence score, coverage guideline summarized parse_status, reportilo_coverage() reports verified-versus-extracted coverage category. Guidelines without machine-readable checklist remain available catalog entries link original source.

title: Export a checklist or flow diagram to a file — reportilo_export
text: x reportilo_checklist, reportilo_flowchart reportilo_rob. file Output file path. extension sets format unless format given. format Optional explicit format (e.g. "docx"). Defaults file extension. ... Passed underlying writer. Flow diagrams risk--bias plots accept background ("white" "transparent"); flow diagrams also accept width, risk--bias accepts type. strict flow diagrams: TRUE, refuse export flowchart_consistency() reports issues (otherwise warning issued file still written). Default FALSE. complete flow diagrams: passed flowchart_consistency(). TRUE, also treat -accounted conservation stages issues (use final, fully filled diagram). Combine strict = TRUE block export final diagram balance exactly. Default FALSE.

title: Export a checklist or flow diagram to a file — reportilo_export
text: output file path, invisibly.

title: Export a checklist or flow diagram to a file — reportilo_export
text: Supported formats: Checklist docx, xlsx, csv. Flow diagram png, svg, pdf, docx, xlsx (counts), csv (counts). Risk bias png, svg, pdf, docx, xlsx (table), csv (table). Pass type = "traffic_light" (default) type = "summary", background = "transparent" background. Word Excel export need suggested packages officer + flextable openxlsx respectively; image export needs DiagrammeR, DiagrammeRsvg rsvg.

title: Export a checklist or flow diagram to a file — reportilo_export

title: Create a risk-of-bias assessment — reportilo_rob
text: Build risk--bias object table per-study, per-domain judgments, ready plot rob_traffic_light() / rob_summary() export reportilo_export(). .data.frame() returns wide judgment table (one row per study, one column per domain plus Overall).

title: Create a risk-of-bias assessment — reportilo_rob
code: reportilo_rob(tool, data = NULL)  # S3 method for class 'reportilo_rob' as.data.frame(x, row.names = NULL, optional = FALSE, ...)

title: Create a risk-of-bias assessment — reportilo_rob
text: tool tool_id (see rob_tools_available()). data wide data frame: study-name column (Study) plus one column per domain id (optionally Overall). NULL, bundled example tool used. Get blank one rob_template(). x reportilo_rob object. row.names, optional Ignored; S3 consistency. ... Ignored.

title: Create a risk-of-bias assessment — reportilo_rob
text: object class reportilo_rob.

text: domains risk--bias tool (see rob_tools).

title: Risk-of-bias tool domains — rob_domains
text: data frame columns: tool_id Tool identifier. domain_id Domain id within tool (e.g. "D1"). label Full domain description. domain_order Display order.

title: Risk-of-bias tool domains — rob_domains
text: robvis underlying tool manuals.

title: Example risk-of-bias assessments — rob_example
text: small example assessment tool, used seed reportilo_rob().

text: data frame long format columns: tool_id Tool identifier. study Study label. domain_id Domain id ("Overall"). judgment judgment level.

title: Example risk-of-bias assessments — rob_example
text: reportilo (illustrative).

title: Risk-of-bias judgment levels — rob_levels
text: judgment levels used across tools, color symbol used render (following robvis).

title: Risk-of-bias judgment levels — rob_levels

text: robvis color scheme.

text: x reportilo_rob reportilo_rob().

title: Risk-of-bias summary plot — rob_summary
text: ggplot object.

title: reportilo 0.1.0
text: First development release. Package skeleton: documentation, tests, continuous integration (R-CMD-check) pkgdown website. launch_reportilo() starts bundled Shiny application. flowchart_consistency() gains complete argument: complete = TRUE requires exact accounting (silently unaccounted records) every conservation stage, stages whose removals/exclusions fully specified split-stages (parts must sum whole, e.g. CONSORT randomized = intervention arm + control arm, per-arm received splits). default remains bounds-, suited draft. reportilo_export() gains matching complete argument flow diagrams; combined strict = TRUE blocks export final diagram balance exactly. Shiny app exposes “Final diagram” toggle. Checklist exports now carry provenance: Word files gain verification line, Excel files gain “Provenance” sheet, exporting auto-extracted (hand-verified) checklist format warns item verified original guideline. Bundled data rights documented inst/COPYRIGHTS (referenced Copyright field), cran-comments.md records provenance licensing basis submission. bundled Shiny app gains shinytest2 smoke test (launch, tab switching, checklist fill, flow diagram consistency warning export, risk--bias preview), run CI headless Chrome skipped CRAN. navbar named (id = "main") active tab addressable. Subsequent pull requests add EQUATOR guideline catalog checklist data, checklist flow diagram API, Word / Excel / image export engine, full Shiny application companion browser application.
